source: Analysis of 5 ETS reference sets (35 sentences) from goarno.io
description: Flavor model for Listen & Repeat sentence generation. Defines the statistical profile of authentic ETS-style repeat sets.

examples: Scripts are on the front table.
Insurance coverage is included in the base price.
We will run through the first act without stopping.
The digital catalog can be accessed from any campus computer.
If you experience any issues with the campus Wi-Fi, please submit a support ticket online.
Late returns will result in an extra daily charge added to your credit card.

easy.notes: Single clause, no subordination, familiar vocabulary

medium.notes: One main clause, may have prepositional or participial extension

hard.notes: Two clauses, conditional logic, consequence structures, multiple parts

challenges: reduced syllables in longer words (comfortable, automatically)
stress placement in compound nouns (study room, front counter)

hard.focus: connected speech, rhythm, and complex phonotactics
challenges: final consonant clusters at phrase boundaries (-nts in, -lts will)
reduced function words in rapid sequences (for our, to your)
conditional intonation pattern (if X, then Y)

scenario_requirements.speaker_role: staff or authority figure giving instructions/orientation
scenario_requirements.register: professional but approachable, clear enunciation expected
scenario_requirements.diversity: no two consecutive sets should use the same scenario type

natural_spoken.description: Sentences sound like spoken instructions, not written text

progressive_difficulty.description: Clear escalation from simple to complex within each set

scenario_coherence.description: All 7 sentences plausibly belong to the same orientation/briefing

timing_appropriate.description: Each sentence can be comfortably spoken within its time allocation
